title: Three Miscarriages - Recurrent Pregnancy Loss
chief_complaint: 33-year-old with 3 consecutive first-trimester miscarriages at 7, 8, and 9 weeks. All confirmed on ultrasound with fetal cardiac activity initially, then demise. No living children.

hidden_data.apla: Lupus anticoagulant: Positive (2 occasions, 12 weeks apart)

key_learning_points: APS: LA + aCL or anti-β2-GPI on 2 occasions
Treatment: LDA + LMWH (prophylactic dose) during pregnancy
Increases live birth rate from 20% to 70-80%
Screen for APS in all women with RPL

patient_responses.losses: Three miscarriages, all after seeing heartbeat

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! APS is a treatable cause of RPL.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is antiphospholipid syndrome. The positive LA + aCL confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: APS in pregnancy: LDA 81mg daily + LMWH (enoxaparin 40mg daily) from positive pregnancy test until 6 weeks postpartum. Increases live birth rate significantly. Monitor for preeclampsia and IUGR.
